economic_notes: First Nation has multiple government business enterprises including Smelqmix Investments LP, Lower Similkameen Community Forests LP, Skulqalt Forestry Ltd, and others. Subsequent to year end, acquired property in Cawston valued at $2,250,000. Church damaged by fire in June 2021 with insurance proceeds of $103,000 approved.
notes: Fiscal year is March 31, 2021, not 2024. Six entities fully consolidated and six government business enterprises accounted for on modified equity basis. Total government transfers calculated as ISC $4,215,038 + CMHC $115,414 + Environment Canada $198,689 + FNESC $276,686 + FNHA $1,322,403 + Province of BC $2,990,018 + Property tax $278,051 + net deferred revenue adjustment (-$1,795,003) approximated; alternatively summing direct government transfer lines: ISC+CMHC+EnvCan+FNESC+FNHA+BC = 9,118,248 plus property tax 278,051 = 9,396,299, net deferred revenue recognized $760,130 not separately split by source. OSR estimated as total revenue minus identified government transfer lines. No remuneration schedule included in the financial statements provided. Subsequent events include church fire and $2.25M property acquisition. Deferred revenue increased significantly due to new school construction funding from BC government.
